title: Phase 4: Intelligence & Handoff
description: Policy engine (TOML config, auto_confirm, yield_to_operator, forbidden), yield engine (silence + prompt detection), operator handoff (state machine, Unix socket, CLI attach, credential-blind return). The decision escalation chain: policy → LLM → operator.

title: Policy Config — TOML parsing for policy rules
description:
Parse policy section of mish.toml into PolicyConfig struct.

Files: src/policy/config.rs

Schema:
[[policy.auto_confirm]] match, respond, scope (optional)
[[policy.yield_to_operator]] match, notify
[[policy.forbidden]] pattern, action, message
[handoff] timeout_sec, fallback
[yield] silence_timeout_ms, prompt_patterns
[watch_presets] name = regex
[timeout_defaults] default, [timeout_defaults.scope] tool = seconds

Types: PolicyConfig { auto_confirm: Vec<AutoConfirmRule>, yield_to_operator: Vec<YieldToOperatorRule>, forbidden: Vec<ForbiddenRule>, handoff: HandoffConfig }
AutoConfirmRule { match_pattern: Regex, respond: String, scope: Option<Vec<String>> }
YieldToOperatorRule { match_pattern: Regex, notify: bool }
ForbiddenRule { pattern: Regex, action: String, message: String }

Tests: Valid TOML→all rules populated. Missing scope→applies globally. Invalid regex→config error. Empty policy→empty rules (not error). Watch presets parsed.
close_reason: CompiledPolicy with regex compilation, 8 tests, load_config_from_str exposed

title: Policy Matcher — rule evaluation with scope and precedence
description:
Match commands/prompts against policy rules. Evaluate precedence: forbidden → yield_to_operator → auto_confirm → fall through to LLM.

Files: src/policy/matcher.rs, src/policy/scope.rs

Scope matching (scope.rs): Extract command binary name (first token, basename only). scope=["npm"] matches "npm install" and "/usr/bin/npm run". No scope→matches all (warn). Known limitations: "env npm"→scope is "env", "sudo apt"→scope is "sudo".

Matcher: evaluate(command, prompt_text, config) → PolicyDecision { Forbidden(msg), YieldToOperator(notify), AutoConfirm(response), FallThroughToLlm }

Precedence: forbidden checked BEFORE execution. yield_to_operator and auto_confirm checked WHEN yield detected (prompt). auto_confirm requires scope match.

Tests: Forbidden "rm -rf /"→blocked. Forbidden "rm file"→not matched. yield_to_operator "Password:"→operator. auto_confirm "Proceed?" scope=npm, cmd=npm→Y. auto_confirm scope mismatch→no match. Precedence correct. Unscoped auto_confirm→logged warning.

title: Yield Detector — silence + prompt heuristic detection
description:
Detect when a running process is waiting for interactive input.

Files: src/yield_engine/detector.rs, src/yield_engine/syscall.rs (Linux-only, cfg-gated)

Algorithm (every 100ms while process running):
1. Read bytes, reset silence timer on receive
2. If silence > silence_timeout_ms (2500ms default):
   a. Check last 256 bytes against prompt_patterns
   b. Match → YIELD
   c. No match → continue
3. On YIELD:
   a. Check policy.yield_to_operator → handoff
   b. Check policy.auto_confirm (+ scope) → send response
   c. No match → state=awaiting_input, surface in digest

Prompt patterns (configurable): [?]$, :$, >$, Password, passphrase, [y/N], [Y/n]

Edge cases: Silence timer only activates after first byte (no false yield on slow startup). Atomic state transition running→awaiting_input via CAS (prevents race with sh_interact send). Session mutex serializes yield + tool calls.

Linux-only (optional): /proc/{pid}/syscall check for read(0,...) — gated behind cfg(target_os="linux"), disabled by default.

Digest entry when awaiting_input: { alias, state:"awaiting_input", prompt_tail, awaiting_since_ms, action_hint }

Tests: Prompt after silence→yield. Silent no prompt→no yield. Slow startup (no output)→no false yield. Auto-confirm match→response sent. yield_to_operator→handoff. No policy→awaiting_input in digest. Concurrent send + yield→mutex serializes.

title: Handoff State Machine — state transitions + crypto IDs
description:
State machine for operator handoff lifecycle.

Files: src/handoff/state.rs

States: running → awaiting_input → handed_off → running

Handoff ID: Crypto-random 128-bit (rand::thread_rng() uses OsRng). Single-use: invalidated after first attachment. Format: hf_<hex-128-bit>. Separate from LLM reference ID (LLM gets reference for status, not attachment credential).

Initiation paths:
A. LLM-initiated: sh_interact(action="handoff", reason="MFA required")
B. Policy-initiated: yield_to_operator rule matches
C. Preemptive: notify_operator=true on sh_spawn

HandoffState { handoff_id, reference_id, alias, reason, initiated_at, attached: bool, operator_pid }

Tests: Create handoff→crypto-random ID. Attach→marks attached. Second attach→error "already attached". Timeout→execute fallback (yield_to_llm|kill|wait). Process exit during handoff→ends handoff. Operator detach→state=running, handoff_resolved in digest.

title: CLI Attach — Unix socket + mish attach/handoffs commands
description:
Operator attachment to handed-off processes via Unix domain socket.

Files: src/handoff/attach.rs, src/cli/management.rs (mish attach, mish handoffs)

Unix domain socket:
- Path: $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/mish/<server-pid>/control.sock
- Mode: 0600 (owner-only)
- Created at server startup

Protocol:
1. Operator runs "mish attach hf_..."
2. Client connects to Unix socket, sends handoff_id
3. Server validates: exists, not expired, not attached
4. Server forwards PTY master fd to client
5. Client sets terminal to raw mode, proxies I/O
6. On detach (Ctrl-\ or exit): client sends detach, server recaptures

"mish handoffs" command: List active handoffs (alias, reason, duration, status). --watch flag polls every 5s, prints new handoffs.

"mish attach" command: Connect to PTY, raw mode I/O proxying.

Tests: Attach valid ID→connected. Invalid ID→error. Expired→error. Already attached→error. Detach→server recaptures. "mish handoffs"→lists active.

title: Credential-Blind Return — handoff summary without output
description:
When operator detaches, return summary WITHOUT process output from handoff period. Prevents credential leakage via process echo.

Files: src/handoff/summary.rs

Default (credential-blind): { handoff_resolved: { duration_ms, lines_during_handoff, outcome:"resolved" } }. No process output content.

Opt-in: "mish attach --share-output" includes process output from handoff period.

Process exit during handoff: outcome="process_exited".

Tests: Default→no output content, only duration + line count. --share-output→includes output. Process exit→outcome "process_exited".

title: Dangerous Handler Policy Integration — route through policy in MCP mode
description:
Update dangerous handler to route through policy engine in MCP mode.

Files: src/handlers/dangerous.rs (update)

Flow (MCP mode):
1. Command matches dangerous pattern
2. Check policy.forbidden → block if matched (error response with message)
3. If not forbidden, return structured warning with command details
4. LLM decides to proceed or abort
5. If LLM proceeds, execute through normal handler

Also update interactive handler (src/handlers/interactive.rs) to check policy for interactive commands in MCP mode.

Tests: Forbidden command→blocked, never executed. Dangerous not forbidden→warning, LLM can proceed. No policy config→basic warning. Interactive+policy→checked.

title: Per-Scope Timeout Config — TOML timeout defaults by command
description:
TOML-based timeout defaults per command scope.

Files: src/policy/config.rs (extend), src/tools/sh_run.rs (use), src/tools/sh_spawn.rs (use)

Config: [timeout_defaults] default=300, [timeout_defaults.scope] terraform=1800, docker=600, cargo=600, npm=300, pip=300

Integration: sh_run and sh_spawn use scope-specific timeout if no explicit timeout parameter provided. Scope matching uses same logic as policy scope (command binary basename).

Tests: terraform→1800s. Unknown→300s default. Explicit timeout param→overrides scope. Scope resolution matches policy scope logic.

title: Phase 5: CLI Proxy Mode
description: The other half of the binary: mish <command> wraps every command with category-aware structured output. CLI entry point, 4 output modes (human, json, passthrough, context), signal handling, stdin forwarding, compound command handling.

title: CLI Entry Point — parse command, detect tool, route
description:
Parse mish <command> arguments, detect tool via grammar, enter category router, produce terminal output.

Files: src/cli/proxy.rs, src/main.rs (CLI dispatch)

Parsing:
- "mish npm install lodash" → command=["npm","install","lodash"]
- "mish --json npm test" → output_mode=JSON, command=["npm","test"]
- "mish --passthrough cargo build" → output_mode=Passthrough
- "mish --context ..." → output_mode=Context

Flow: Parse CLI args → detect tool → category router → handler → format output → print to terminal

Integration: Uses router/mod.rs route() with mode=Cli. Uses format.rs for output formatting.

Tests: Simple command parsing. Flag extraction (--json, --passthrough, --context). Unknown flags passed through to command.

title: Output Formatting — human, json, passthrough, context modes
description:
Multiple output modes for different consumers.

Files: src/core/format.rs

Human mode (default): Status symbols (+, !, ~, →, ⚠). Indented lines for details.
Example: "1400 lines → exit 0 (12.3s)\n + 147 packages installed\n ~ npm warn deprecated (x3)"

JSON mode (--json): Structured JSON with command, exit_code, elapsed, total_lines, outcomes[], hazards[].

Passthrough mode (--passthrough): Full output streams through in real time. Summary appended at end: "── mish summary ──\n{condensed}"

Context mode (--context): Maximum compression for LLM context: "npm install: ok 147pkg 12.3s ~deprecated(x3) ~2vuln"

Tests: Human→correct symbols. JSON→valid JSON, all fields. Passthrough→output streams, summary appended. Context→maximally compressed.

title: Signal Handling + stdin Forwarding — CLI process management
description:
Proper signal proxying and user input forwarding for CLI mode.

Files: src/cli/proxy.rs (event loop), src/core/pty.rs (signal forwarding)

Signals:
- SIGINT → forward to child. Second SIGINT within 1s → mish exits too.
- SIGTERM → forward to child, wait, finalize.
- SIGWINCH → resize PTY dimensions via TIOCSWINSZ ioctl.
- SIGTSTP → forward to child (Ctrl-Z suspend).
- SIGCONT → forward to child (resume).
- SIGCHLD → child exited, trigger drain + finalize.

stdin forwarding: Read user stdin, write to PTY master fd unmodified. Critical for interactive prompts (password entry, y/n questions).

Event loop: poll PTY for output, poll stdin for input, timer events (partial line 500ms, silence 2s, flush 5s).

Tests: SIGWINCH→PTY resized. stdin forwarded correctly. Double SIGINT→mish exits. SIGTERM→graceful finalize.

title: Compound Command Handling — pipes and && chains
description:
Handle pipes and compound operators in CLI proxy mode.

Files: src/cli/proxy.rs (parsing), src/router/mod.rs (multi-segment routing)

Pipes: "mish \"cat file | grep error\"" → shell spawns full pipeline as unit, category=passthrough (final output is what matters).

Compound: "mish \"cd /proj && npm install && npm test\"" → split on && / || / ;, handle each segment with its own category:
- cd /proj → narrate
- npm install → condense
- npm test → condense
Combined output: "→ cd: /proj\n+ npm install: 147 packages\n+ npm test: 42 passed"

Splitting rules: && stops on failure, || continues on failure, ; unconditional sequential.

Tests: Quoted pipeline→unit. && chain→sequential, stops on failure. || chain→continues. ; chain→unconditional. Mixed operators.

title: CLI Integration Tests — real commands through proxy
description:
End-to-end tests running real commands through the full CLI proxy pipeline.

Files: tests/ (integration test files)

Test commands covering all 6 categories:
- Passthrough: "mish echo hello", "mish ls -la", "mish cat /etc/hosts"
- Narrate: "mish cp /dev/null /tmp/mish_test", "mish mkdir -p /tmp/mish_test_dir"
- Structured: "mish git status" (in a git repo)
- Condense: Would need a build command (optional/mocked)
- Interactive: "mish vim" → verify error/transparent handling
- Dangerous: "mish rm -rf /tmp/mish_test_dir" → verify warning

Output mode tests: --json produces valid JSON. --passthrough streams + summary.

Error case: command that fails → enrichment appears.

Tests: Each category exercised with real command. JSON output verified. Failed command→enrichment.

title: Code Review Remediation
description: Fix all code review findings before mish-b1j.21 (Integration Tests). 4 reviewers found 19 issues across architecture, concurrency, Rust idiom, and general quality. 10 task beads in 2 waves.
close_reason: All 10 code review remediation beads complete. 19 issues fixed across architecture, concurrency, Rust idioms, and quality.

title: Fix MCP Tool Schema Mismatches
description: P0 CORRECTNESS BUG. sh_interact schema advertises "send" but handler matches "send_input". sh_session schema advertises only "list" but handler supports "create" and "close". Align inputSchema JSON in dispatch.rs with actual match arms in sh_interact::handle() and sh_session::handle(). Files: src/mcp/dispatch.rs. Verify: cargo test passes, every handler match arm appears in its schema.
close_reason: Fixed: sh_interact schema (send→send_input, signal→send_signal, +read_full), sh_session schema (+create, +close, +name, +shell properties). 3 regression tests added.

title: Observability + Stub Fixes
description: P3 OBSERVABILITY. Two fixes: (1) Add AuditEvent::ToolCall variant with tool name, cmd, exit_code. Log in McpDispatcher::dispatch() after tool returns. (2) In main.rs, change mish attach and mish handoffs stubs to exit non-zero (std::process::exit(1)) instead of silent success. Files: src/audit/logger.rs, src/mcp/dispatch.rs, src/main.rs. Verify: cargo test, audit events include tool calls.
close_reason: Completed: AuditEvent::ToolCall variant with logging in dispatch, attach/handoffs stubs exit non-zero. All tests pass.

title: Wire Safety Deny List in sh_run and sh_spawn
description: P0 SAFETY. safety::check_deny_list() exists with 7 deny patterns but is never called. At top of sh_run::handle(), call safety::check_deny_list(&cmd) — if Some(reason), return ToolError. Same for sh_spawn::handle(). Add tests: sh_run with "rm -rf /" returns error; sh_spawn with "mkfs.ext4 /dev/sda" returns error. Files: src/tools/sh_run.rs, src/tools/sh_spawn.rs, src/safety.rs.
close_reason: Wired safety::check_deny_list() into sh_run and sh_spawn. ERR_COMMAND_BLOCKED on match. 5 tests added.

title: Wire sh_run Through Category Router
description: P1 ARCHITECTURE. sh_run::handle() bypasses category router — commands go straight to session execute and unconditionally through squasher. Fix: call router::route() on the command, dispatch to appropriate handler by category. Only squash for Category::Condense. Return structured output for Category::Structured. Handle Category::Dangerous with deny-list. Files: src/tools/sh_run.rs, src/router/mod.rs. Depends on: schema fix (bead 1). Verify: cargo test, sh_run("ls") squashed, sh_run("git status") structured, sh_run("cp a b") passthrough.
close_reason: Wired sh_run through category router. Categorizes commands, only squashes for Condense, blocks Dangerous, reports category in response. 8 new tests, 789 total passing.

title: Fix Shutdown Race — Await JoinHandle
description: P1 CONCURRENCY. Shutdown task tokio::spawned but JoinHandle never stored/awaited. Main can return before sessions cleaned up, leaking PTY fds and creating zombies. Fix: store JoinHandle from tokio::spawn, await it after run_with_shutdown exits, before cleanup block. Files: src/mcp/server.rs. Verify: cargo test, existing shutdown tests pass.
close_reason: Stored shutdown JoinHandle + awaited with 5s timeout. All 12 server tests pass.

title: Fix Blocking Sleep in Async Context
description: P1 CONCURRENCY. PtyCapture::wait() uses std::thread::sleep(10ms) in busy loop — blocks tokio worker threads, causes thread starvation under parallel tool calls. Fix option: wrap call sites with tokio::task::spawn_blocking (lower risk since wait() also called from non-async contexts and Drop). Files: src/core/pty.rs. Verify: cargo test, all PTY tests pass.
close_reason: Added wait_async() using spawn_blocking. Sync wait() preserved for non-async callers + Drop. Test passes.

title: Release Mutex Before wait_for Poll Loop
description: P1 CONCURRENCY. dispatch_sh_spawn holds process_table.lock() for entire wait_for poll duration (up to 300s). All other tool calls blocked. Fix: acquire mutex for setup, release before poll loop, access spool via its own Arc, re-acquire mutex for digest. May require splitting sh_spawn::handle() into handle_spawn() and handle_wait(). Depends on: bead 3 (router). Files: src/mcp/dispatch.rs, src/tools/sh_spawn.rs. Verify: cargo test, concurrent sh_spawn + sh_help dont deadlock.
close_reason: Split sh_spawn into setup() + wait_for_match(). Lock held only during registration, released before poll loop. Concurrent sh_help completes in <500ms while wait_for polls. 790 tests passing.

title: Fix Zombie Reaping + Schedule Cleanup Tasks
description: P2 RESOURCE MGMT. Two issues: (1) PtyCapture::Drop uses WNOHANG so still-running children become zombies — add SIGTERM+wait or reaper thread. (2) cleanup_expired() and cleanup_idle_sessions() exist but never called — spawn periodic cleanup task in run_server() with 30s interval. Files: src/core/pty.rs, src/process/table.rs, src/session/manager.rs, src/mcp/server.rs. Verify: cargo test, no zombie accumulation.
close_reason: Drop: SIGTERM→wait→SIGKILL reaping. run_server: 30s cleanup task for expired processes + idle sessions.

title: Consolidate Duplicated Types
description: P2 DRY. Four duplications: (1) ToolError defined in 4 tool modules — move to tools/mod.rs with Display+Error impls. (2) OutputMode defined twice with identity conversion — keep in core/format.rs, import in preflight.rs, delete to_preflight_mode(). (3) expand_tilde() defined 3 times with behavioral inconsistency — move to core/paths.rs (new file). (4) Error codes as magic integers in process/table.rs and session/manager.rs — import constants from mcp/types.rs. Files: src/tools/{mod,sh_run,sh_spawn,sh_interact,sh_session}.rs, src/core/{format,preflight}.rs, src/cli/proxy.rs, src/config.rs, src/config_loader.rs, src/audit/logger.rs, src/process/table.rs, src/session/manager.rs. Verify: cargo test, grep confirms exactly 1 definition each.
close_reason: Completed: 4 type consolidations — ToolError, OutputMode, expand_tilde, magic integers. Net -106 lines. 35 tests pass.

title: Type Safety + Idiom Improvements
description: P3 IDIOM. Five fixes: (1) Create InteractAction and SessionAction enums in mcp/types.rs with serde rename_all snake_case, update handler match arms. (2) Add impl Display for Category in router/categories.rs, delete category_to_str() from proxy.rs. (3) Either remove initialized field from McpDispatcher or add guard rejecting tool calls before init. (4) Replace AtomicU64 with plain u64 in ProcessTable (already behind TokioMutex). (5) Use std::sync::OnceLock for preset regex in sh_run.rs. Depends on: bead 8 (consolidation). Files: src/mcp/types.rs, src/mcp/dispatch.rs, src/router/categories.rs, src/cli/proxy.rs, src/process/table.rs, src/tools/sh_run.rs. Verify: cargo test + cargo clippy clean.
close_reason: Completed: InteractAction/SessionAction enums, Display for Category, initialized guard, AtomicU64→u64, OnceLock for regex. 791 tests pass.

title: Phase 2: MCP Server Mode
description: Full MCP server (mish serve) with 5 tools, session management, process table, config system. Shares Phase 1 core (router, squasher, PTY, handlers). 12 components, ~20 task beads.
close_reason: Phase 2 complete: all 21 beads closed. MCP server mode fully implemented — 5 tools, session management, process table, config system, integration tests, code review remediation.

title: Config System
description: src/config.rs — Parse mish.toml with all sections (server, squasher, yield, timeout_defaults, watch_presets, audit, policy/handoff/sandbox stubs). load_config(), default_config(), validate_config(). Tilde expansion, forward-compat unknown sections. See manifest Component 1.
close_reason: Config system with TOML parsing, 18 tests

title: Shell Process Lifecycle
description: src/session/shell.rs — ShellProcess: spawn interactive non-login shell in PTY (bash -i / zsh -i). Inject boundary hooks at startup. Discard startup output (motd, rc). Detect shell readiness via first boundary sentinel. execute_command(cmd) -> writes cmd, blocks until boundary detected, returns output + exit code + new CWD. Uses PtyCapture from Phase 1. See manifest Component 3.
close_reason: Shell Process Lifecycle: PTY shell spawn, boundary-based command execution, CWD tracking. 10 tests.

title: Session Manager
description: src/session/manager.rs — SessionManager: create_session(name) -> Session, get/close/list sessions. Enforces max_sessions limit. Per-session mutex for serialized command execution. Idle timeout cleanup (configurable, default 1hr). Session tracks: name, shell process, CWD, created_at, last_used, process history. See manifest Component 3.
close_reason: Implemented: SessionManager with 18 tests. create/get/list/close/cleanup, per-session mutex, idle timeout, MCP error codes.

title: sh_run Tool
description: src/tools/sh_run.rs — Synchronous command execution. Params: command (required), session, timeout, watch, output_mode. Sends command to session shell, waits for boundary, routes through Phase 1 category router, returns squashed output. Watch pattern filtering via PatternMatcher. Timeout enforcement with configurable defaults. Returns: RouterResult formatted as MCP response + process digest. See manifest Component 5.
close_reason: Implemented: sh_run tool with category router, squasher, watch patterns, timeout resolution. 33 tests.

title: sh_spawn Tool
description: src/tools/sh_spawn.rs — Background process management. Params: command (required), alias (optional, auto-generated), session, wait_for (regex to match before returning), watch, timeout. Registers process in ProcessTable with state tracking. Alias uniqueness enforcement (-32004 on conflict). wait_for blocks until regex matches or timeout. Returns: pid, alias, initial output, matched lines, process digest. See manifest Component 5.
close_reason: Implemented: sh_spawn tool with ProcessTable, alias management, wait_for regex. 23 tests.

title: sh_interact Tool
description: src/tools/sh_interact.rs — Process interaction. Params: alias (required), action (read_tail/read_full/send_input/send_signal/kill/status). read_tail: last N lines from spool. read_full: entire spool. send_input: write string to process stdin. send_signal: SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGSTOP/SIGCONT/SIGHUP. kill: SIGKILL+cleanup. status: state+elapsed+exit_code. State validation: -32009 on invalid action for state. See manifest Component 5.
close_reason: Implemented: sh_interact tool with 6 actions, state validation. 38 tests.

title: sh_session Tool
description: src/tools/sh_session.rs — Session lifecycle. Params: action (create/list/close), name (required for create/close), shell (optional: bash/zsh/sh). create: spawns ShellProcess, returns session info + CWD. list: returns all sessions with stats. close: kills all session processes, removes session. Error codes: -32002 not found, -32006 limit reached, -32008 already closed. See manifest Component 5.
close_reason: Implemented: sh_session tool with create/list/close CRUD. 17 tests.

title: sh_help Tool
description: src/tools/sh_help.rs — Self-documenting reference card. Params: tool (optional filter). Returns markdown-formatted help for all 5 tools or filtered by name. Includes: parameter tables, response schemas, watch preset list from config, example invocations. Dynamic: includes current session count, process count from live state. See manifest Component 5.
close_reason: Implemented: sh_help self-documenting reference card. 18 tests.

title: MCP Dispatch
description: src/mcp/dispatch.rs — MCP protocol handler. handle_request(Request) dispatches: initialize (returns capabilities + tool list), tools/list (returns 5 tool schemas), tools/call (routes to sh_run/sh_spawn/sh_interact/sh_session/sh_help). Attaches process table digest to every tools/call response. Error mapping: tool errors -> JSON-RPC errors with correct codes. See manifest Component 2.
close_reason: Implemented: McpDispatcher with initialize, tools/list (5 schemas), tools/call routing to all 5 tools, notifications, process digest on all responses, error mapping. 13 tests.

title: Graceful Shutdown
description: src/shutdown.rs — ShutdownManager: signal handler (SIGTERM/SIGINT), drain timeout, session cleanup. On shutdown: stop accepting new requests, wait for in-flight requests (5s drain), close all sessions (kill processes), flush audit log, exit. Crash recovery: on startup, detect stale PID file, clean up orphaned PTY fds. See manifest Component 8.
close_reason: Implemented: ShutdownManager with signal handling, drain, PID files, crash recovery. 20 tests.

title: MCP Server + CLI Management
description: src/mcp/server.rs + src/cli/management.rs — Server: main event loop reading MCP transport, dispatching requests, managing lifecycle. Startup sequence: load config, init audit logger, init session manager, init process table, start transport loop. CLI management: mish ps (list sessions/processes), mish logs (tail audit log), mish config check (validate TOML). See manifest Components 8, 9, 10.
close_reason: Implemented: MCP server event loop (server.rs) + CLI management commands (management.rs). 26 tests, all passing.

title: MCP Types + Error Codes
description: src/mcp/types.rs — All JSON-RPC types: Request/Response/Error structs, all 5 tool param/result structs (ShRunParams, ShSpawnParams, ShInteractParams, ShSessionParams, ShHelpParams + results), ProcessDigestEntry with conditional fields, 9 app error codes (-32001 to -32009). Serde derive for all. See manifest Component 2.
close_reason: MCP types + error codes, 39 tests

title: Main + lib.rs Wiring
description: src/main.rs + src/lib.rs updates — Wire up all new modules in lib.rs (pub mod mcp, session, process, tools, config, config_loader, safety, audit, shutdown). Update main.rs: Serve subcommand starts MCP server, Ps/Logs subcommands call CLI management, Proxy subcommand uses RuntimeConfig from config_loader instead of empty defaults. See manifest Components 9, 11.
close_reason: Wired main.rs: Serve→run_server, Ps/Logs/Config Check→management, Proxy→RuntimeConfig. Updated 2 integration tests.

title: Integration Tests
description: tests/mcp_integration.rs — End-to-end MCP server tests. Spawn mish serve as subprocess, send JSON-RPC requests via stdin, verify responses. Test categories: sh_session create/list/close lifecycle, sh_run with real commands through category router, sh_spawn + sh_interact background process lifecycle, process table digest presence on every response, error codes for invalid requests, graceful shutdown on EOF. See manifest Component 12.
close_reason: Closed

title: Process Primitives
description: src/process/state.rs + src/process/spool.rs — State machine (7 states: Spawning, Running, AwaitingInput, Paused, Exited, Killed, HandedOff) with valid transitions and atomic CAS. Raw output spool: per-process circular buffer (1MB default), append/read_tail/read_full, aggregate limit enforcement (50MB). See manifest Component 4.
close_reason: Process state machine + output spool, 42 tests

title: Safety + Deny-list
description: src/safety.rs — Command deny-list checking. check_denied(command, deny_list) -> Option<DeniedReason>. Static deny patterns: rm -rf /, mkfs, dd if= of=/dev, :(){ :|:& };:. Regex-based matching against full command string. Log denied attempts. See manifest Component 6.
close_reason: Safety deny-list, 15 tests

title: Config Loader
description: src/config_loader.rs — RuntimeConfig struct aggregating MishConfig + grammars + categories + dangerous_patterns. load_runtime_config() reads: bundled grammars via include_str!, user grammars from ~/.config/mish/grammars/, _meta/categories.toml, _meta/dangerous.toml. Fixes Phase 1 gap (CLI proxy uses empty defaults). Both modes share this. See manifest Component 1.
close_reason: Config Loader: RuntimeConfig aggregating grammars + categories + dangerous_patterns + MishConfig. 11 tests.

title: MCP Transport
description: src/mcp/transport.rs — Stdio JSON-RPC transport. McpTransport struct reading newline-delimited JSON from stdin, writing to stdout. recv() -> Option<Request>, send(Response), send_error(). EOF detection for shutdown. Serde JSON serialization. See manifest Component 2.
close_reason: MCP Transport: StdioTransport with async JSON-RPC over stdio. 16 tests.

title: Audit Logger
description: src/audit/logger.rs — Append-only audit log. AuditLogger::new(config) opens log file (create dirs, O_APPEND). log_command(), log_policy_decision(), log_session_event(), log_error(). Structured format: timestamp level category details. Rotation: basic size check, rename .1. fd must NOT be inherited by child processes (CLOEXEC). See manifest Component 7.
close_reason: Audit Logger: append-only JSON Lines log with O_CLOEXEC, log level filtering. 14 tests.

title: Command Boundary Detection
description: src/session/boundary.rs — Detect command completion in interactive shells. Primary: PROMPT_COMMAND/precmd hook injection with UUID sentinel. hook_script_bash() and hook_script_zsh() generate shell code. BoundaryDetector::detect(line) parses sentinel lines to extract exit code and CWD. Fallback: OSC 133 sequence parsing. See manifest Component 3.
close_reason: Command boundary detection, 20 tests

title: Process Table + Digest
description: src/process/table.rs — ProcessTable: register/update/remove/get/list processes. ProcessEntry with pid, alias, state, elapsed, output excerpt. Digest generation: full/changed/none modes with monotonic sequence counter. Lifecycle rules: 5-min retention for terminal, 20-entry cap, compact stubs for seen entries. Ordering: awaiting_input first, then running, then terminal. See manifest Component 4.
close_reason: Process Table + Digest: registration, state tracking, 3 digest modes, compact stubs. 30 tests.

title: Phase 1: Shared Core + CLI Proxy
description:
## Goal
mish <command> works end-to-end with all 6 categories. 5 grammars with test fixtures.

## Deliverables
1. mish <command> runs any command through the category router
2. All 6 categories functional (condense, narrate, passthrough, structured, interactive, dangerous)
3. Squasher pipeline processes verbose output
4. 5 grammars (npm, cargo, git, docker, make) with test fixtures
5. Error enrichment on failure
6. Preflight argument injection
7. Output modes: human, JSON, passthrough, context
8. Integration tests pass

## Critical Gate
Step 2 (PTY stress test) must pass before any other work proceeds.

## Dependency Graph
Step 1 → Steps 2,3 (parallel) → Steps 4,7,8,9 → Steps 5,10,11 → Step 6 → Step 12 → Step 13 → Step 15
Step 3 → Step 14 (parallel with implementation)
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: 15/15 beads closed, 404 tests passing. CLI proxy works end-to-end with all 6 categories, squasher pipeline, 5 grammars, error enrichment, preflight injection, 4 output modes, and 35 integration tests.

title: Step 1: Project Scaffold
description:
## Task
Create Rust workspace with all module stubs.

## Files
- Cargo.toml (with all dependencies from mish_spec.md §17)
- src/main.rs (clap CLI: mish serve | mish <command> | mish attach/ps/handoffs)
- src/lib.rs
- All module directories with stub files:
  src/cli/proxy.rs, src/router/{mod.rs,categories.rs}
  src/handlers/{condense,narrate,passthrough,structured,interactive,dangerous}.rs
  src/squasher/{pipeline,vte_strip,utf8,progress,truncate,pattern,dedup}.rs
  src/core/{pty,line_buffer,classifier,emit,grammar,stat,enrich,preflight,format}.rs
- grammars/_meta/{categories.toml,dangerous.toml,packages.toml}
- grammars/_shared/{ansi-progress,node-stacktrace,python-traceback,c-compiler-output}.toml
- tests/fixtures/

## Dependencies (Cargo.toml)
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
toml = "0.8"
regex = "1"
uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4"] }
nix = { version = "0.29", features = ["process", "signal", "pty", "fs"] }
vte = "0.13"
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = "0.3"
rand = "0.8"
[dev-dependencies]
assert_cmd = "2"
predicates = "3"
tempfile = "3"

## Acceptance Criteria
- cargo build succeeds
- cargo run -- --help shows subcommands
- cargo test passes (empty test suite)

## Spec Source
mish_spec.md §15 Step 1, §17, ARCHITECTURE.md module structure
close_reason: Scaffold complete: cargo build/test/--help all pass. 46 files, 4-layer module structure, clap CLI, grammar stubs.

title: Step 10: Error Enrichment
description:
## Task
Implement error enrichment on command failure.

## Files
- src/core/enrich.rs

## Types
PathDiagnosis { requested: PathBuf, last_valid: PathBuf, breaks_at: PathBuf, siblings: Vec<String> }
FileOpDiagnosis { source_exists: bool, source_info: Option<FileInfo>, dest_exists: bool,
  dest_info: Option<FileInfo>, dest_parent_exists: bool, dest_parent_info: Option<FileInfo> }
PermissionDiagnosis { path: PathBuf, owner: String, group: String, mode: u32,
  running_as: String, running_group: String, issue: String }
CommandDiagnosis { command: String, in_path: bool, package_hint: Option<String> }

## Built-in Diagnostics (15 functions)
source_exists, dest_path_walk, permissions, is_git_repo, branch_exists,
branch_list_similar, working_tree_clean, remote_ref_status, ahead_behind,
port_listening, dir_listing, disk_space, node_modules_check,
command_not_found_hint, command_similar

Cost tags: instant (<1ms), fast (<10ms), moderate (<100ms)
Total budget: <100ms

## Enrichment Triggers
Only on exit code != 0. Read-only. Non-speculative.
Intent mapping: file ops, dir ops, network ops, build/run, process execution.
Exit codes: 126 (permission denied), 127 (command not found), 137 (OOM), 139 (segfault), 130 (SIGINT)

## Output Format
Indented context below error line:
  ! {error} (exit {code})
    {key}: {value}
    {key}: {value}

## Tests
17 tests: path resolution (break point detection), source/target existence,
permission diagnosis, command not found with package hint, budget enforcement,
file op enrichment, git enrichment, network enrichment, exit code mapping

## Spec Source
ENRICH.md (full spec)
close_reason: Implemented error enrichment: 15 diagnostic functions, intent detection, exit code mapping, budget enforcement, grammar integration. 25 tests.

title: Step 11: Preflight (Argument Injection)
description:
## Task
Implement bidirectional argument injection (quiet + verbose flags).

## Files
- src/core/preflight.rs

## Functions
preflight(command: &mut Vec<String>, grammar: &Grammar, mode: OutputMode) -> Vec<Recommendation>
inject_verbosity(command: &mut Vec<String>, grammar: &Grammar)

## Quiet Flags (from grammar [quiet] section)
npm: safe_inject=["--no-progress"], recommend=["--loglevel=warn"]
cargo: recommend=["--message-format=json"]
pip: safe_inject=["--no-color","--progress-bar=off"], recommend=["-q"]
docker build: safe_inject=["--progress=plain"], recommend=["-q"]
curl: safe_inject=["-s"]
terraform: safe_inject=["-no-color"]

## Verbose Flags (from grammar [verbosity] section)
cp/mv/rm/mkdir/chmod/chown/ln: inject=["-v"]
rsync: inject=["-v","--stats"]
curl: inject=["-s","-w '%{http_code} %{size_download}'"]
wget: inject=["-nv"]
git add: inject=["-v"]
git commit: inject=["--stat"]

## Safety Constraints
1. Never inject behavior-changing flags
2. Never inject if already present
3. Never inject in passthrough mode
4. Never inject flags that suppress errors
5. Each injection declared in grammar

## Tests
12 tests: quiet injection, verbose injection, no double injection,
passthrough mode skip, per-action override, recommendation generation,
safety constraint verification, flag position insertion

## Spec Source
PREFLIGHT.md, VERBOSITY.md
close_reason: Preflight argument injection: 15 tests covering quiet/verbose/safety

title: Step 12: Category Router
description:
## Task
Wire up the top-level category router that dispatches to all handlers.

## Files
- src/router/mod.rs

## Function
route(command: &[String], grammars: &Grammars, config: &Config, mode: ExecutionMode) -> Result<RouterResult>
  1. Load grammar for command (detect_tool)
  2. Run preflight if applicable
  3. Categorize command
  4. Dispatch to handler: Condense->condense, Narrate->narrate, Passthrough->passthrough,
     Structured->structured, Interactive->interactive, Dangerous->dangerous
  5. If handler succeeds and exit_code != 0, run error enrichment
  6. Return result

## Tests
11 tests: route npm install -> condense, route cp -> narrate, route cat -> passthrough,
route git status -> structured, route vim -> interactive, route rm -rf -> dangerous,
unknown command -> condense (fallback), preflight integration, enrichment on failure,
grammar override category, compound command routing

## Spec Source
ARCHITECTURE.md (Layer 2), PROXY.md (resolution order)
close_reason: Implemented category router: grammar detection, preflight, categorization, dispatch to 6 handlers, enrichment path. 13 tests.

title: Step 13: CLI Proxy + Format
description:
## Task
Wire CLI entry point and implement output formatting.

## Files
- src/cli/proxy.rs
- src/core/format.rs

## CLI Proxy
Parse command from CLI args, invoke category router, format output to terminal.
Handle compound commands (split on &&, ||, ;).

## Output Formats
Human (default): status symbols (+, !, ~, ?, ->, warning), indented lines
JSON (--json): { command, exit_code, elapsed_seconds, total_lines, outcomes[], hazards[] }
Passthrough (--passthrough): full output + summary footer
Context (--context): ultra-compressed single line

## Status Symbols
+ success, ! error, ~ warning, ? awaiting input, -> narration, warning dangerous

## Tests
17 tests: human format output, JSON format, passthrough mode, context mode,
compound command splitting (&&, ||, ;), exit code propagation,
multiple command results, format symbol correctness

## Spec Source
PROXY.md (response format), STREAMING.md (output modes)
close_reason: Implemented CLI proxy + format: 4 output modes, compound commands, 17 tests, all passing

title: Step 14: 5 Grammars + Test Fixtures
description:
## Task
Author 5 tool grammars and shared grammars with captured real-output test fixtures.

## Grammar Files
grammars/npm.toml -- detect=["npm","npx"], inherit=["ansi-progress","node-stacktrace"]
  Actions: install (detect=["install","i","add","ci"]), test, run
grammars/cargo.toml -- detect=["cargo"], inherit=["ansi-progress"]
  Actions: build, test
grammars/git.toml -- detect=["git"], inherit=["ansi-progress"]
  Actions: push, pull, clone
grammars/docker.toml -- detect=["docker"], inherit=["ansi-progress"]
  Actions: build, up (compose)
grammars/make.toml -- detect=["make","gmake"], inherit=["ansi-progress","c-compiler-output"]
  Fallback action only

## Shared Grammars

grammars/_shared/node-stacktrace.toml -- Node.js stack frames
grammars/_shared/python-traceback.toml -- Python traceback frames
grammars/_shared/c-compiler-output.toml -- compiler command echoes

## Meta Files
grammars/_meta/categories.toml -- full category->command mapping (6 categories)
grammars/_meta/dangerous.toml -- 9 dangerous patterns
grammars/_meta/packages.toml -- command->package hints (rg->ripgrep, fd->fd-find, etc)

## Test Fixtures (captured real output)
tests/fixtures/npm_install.txt
tests/fixtures/cargo_build.txt
tests/fixtures/pytest_output.txt
tests/fixtures/ansi_output.txt
tests/fixtures/interactive_prompt.txt

## Tests
21 tests: each grammar parses successfully, each grammar categorizes correctly,
each grammar's hazard/outcome/noise rules match test fixtures,
shared grammar inheritance works, summary templates produce expected output

## Spec Source
GRAMMARS.md (all grammar TOML content), mish_spec.md §16 (test fixtures)
close_reason: 5 grammars (npm/cargo/git/docker/make) + 5 fixtures + 29 integration tests

title: Step 15: Integration Tests
description:
## Task
End-to-end integration tests through the full CLI proxy pipeline.

## Files
- tests/ directory

## Test Scenarios
1. mish echo "hello" -- passthrough, verify output + metadata footer
2. mish ls -la -- passthrough, verify metadata footer with line count
3. mish cp (with tempfile) -- narrate, verify narration output
4. mish mkdir -p (with tempdir) -- narrate, verify dir creation narration
5. mish rm (with tempfile) -- narrate, verify removal narration
6. mish echo "hello" (condense fallback for unknown) -- condense, verify summary
7. mish git status (in git repo) -- structured, verify structured output
8. Condense with real verbose output (npm install or cargo build fixture)
9. Error enrichment: mish cp nonexistent /tmp/ -- verify path diagnosis
10. Compound command: mish "echo a && echo b" -- verify both outputs
11. Exit code propagation: mish false -- verify exit 1
12. JSON output mode: mish --json echo hello -- verify JSON structure
13. Passthrough mode: mish --passthrough echo hello -- verify full output + summary
14-25: Additional category-specific and edge case tests

## Spec Source
mish_spec.md §15 Step 15, §16 (testing strategy)
close_reason: 35 integration tests passing: basic execution, exit codes, 4 output modes, compound commands, condensation, error handling, file ops, edge cases. Total: 404 tests.

title: Step 2: PTY + Line Buffer (GATE)
description:
## Task
Implement PTY allocation and line buffer. This is the VALIDATION GATE.

## Files
- src/core/pty.rs
- src/core/line_buffer.rs

## Types
PtyCapture { master_fd: RawFd, child_pid: Pid, start_time: Instant }
  Methods: spawn(command) -> Result<Self>, read_output(buf) -> Result<usize>,
  write_stdin(buf) -> Result<usize>, wait() -> Result<ExitStatus>,
  resize(cols, rows) -> Result<()>, signal(sig) -> Result<()>, drain() -> Result<Vec<u8>>

LineBuffer { partial: Vec<u8>, overwrite_mode: bool, last_byte_time: Instant, partial_timeout: Duration }
  Methods: new(), ingest(bytes) -> Vec<Line>, emit_partial() -> Option<Line>, finalize(remaining) -> Vec<Line>

Line enum: Complete(String), Overwrite(String), Partial(String)

fn is_erase_sequence(bytes, pos) -> Option<usize> -- detect CSI K/2K/A/J sequences

## Byte-Level Rules
\n -> emit Complete, clear partial
\r\n -> emit Complete
\r alone -> set overwrite_mode, clear partial
\r then more bytes -> discard previous, accumulate new
No terminator after 500ms -> emit Partial

## GATE: PTY Stress Tests (ALL MUST PASS)
1. ANSI color passthrough (spawn printf with ANSI codes, verify bytes)
2. Progress bar detection (CR without LF, verify overwrite collapse)
3. SIGWINCH forwarding (resize PTY, verify child gets new dimensions)
4. Raw mode detection (for interactive category)
5. Multi-byte UTF-8 at buffer boundaries (split 3-byte char across reads)

## Additional Tests
6-18: LineBuffer unit tests (complete, windows CRLF, multiple lines, partial timeout,
overwrite, progress collapse, CR without content, empty line, finalize, binary data,
CSI erase, CSI cursor up)

## PIVOT DIRECTIVE
If PTY stress test fails on macOS, STOP. Do not fix. Escalate to project owner.
The project may pivot to a real shell implementation.

## Spec Source
ARCHITECTURE.md (PTY Capture, Line Buffer, Platform Risk), STREAMING.md
close_reason: PTY + Line Buffer complete. All 5 GATE stress tests pass on macOS. 25 tests (15 line_buffer + 10 pty). No pivot needed.

title: Step 3: Grammar System + Categories
description:
## Task
Implement TOML grammar loading and category resolution.

## Files
- src/core/grammar.rs
- src/router/categories.rs

## Types
Grammar { tool: ToolInfo, detect: Vec<String>, inherit: Vec<String>, global_noise: Vec<Rule>,
  actions: HashMap<String, Action>, fallback: Option<Action>, quiet: Option<QuietConfig>,
  verbosity: Option<VerbosityConfig>, enrich: Option<EnrichConfig>, category: Option<Category> }
ToolInfo { name: String }
Action { detect: Vec<String>, noise: Vec<Rule>, hazard: Vec<Rule>, outcome: Vec<Rule>, summary: SummaryTemplate }
Rule { pattern: Regex, action: RuleAction, severity: Option<Severity>, captures: Vec<String>, multiline: Option<u32> }
RuleAction: Strip, Dedup, Keep, Promote
SummaryTemplate { success: String, failure: String, partial: String }
Category: Condense, Narrate, Passthrough, Structured, Interactive, Dangerous
DangerousPattern { pattern: Regex, reason: String }
QuietConfig { safe_inject: Vec<String>, recommend: Vec<String>, actions: HashMap<String, QuietActionConfig> }
VerbosityConfig { inject: Vec<String>, provides: Vec<String> }
EnrichConfig { on_failure: Vec<String>, args: Option<EnrichArgMapping>, actions: HashMap<String, EnrichActionConfig> }

## Functions
load_grammar(path) -> Result<Grammar>
load_all_grammars(grammars_dir) -> Result<HashMap<String, Grammar>>
detect_tool(args, grammars) -> Option<(Grammar, Option<Action>)>
resolve_action(grammar, args) -> Option<&Action>
categorize(command, grammars, categories_config, dangerous_patterns) -> Category
load_categories_config(path) -> Result<CategoriesConfig>
load_dangerous_patterns(path) -> Result<Vec<DangerousPattern>>
format_summary(grammar, outcomes, exit_code) -> Vec<String>

## Category Resolution Order
1. Dangerous patterns (regex match against full command string) — FIRST
2. Grammar front matter category field
3. categories.toml mapping
4. Fallback to Condense

## Rule Evaluation Order (within grammar)
1. Hazard rules (never suppress errors)
2. Outcome rules (extract summary info)
3. Noise rules (strip or dedup)
4. No match -> falls to universal patterns

## Tests
1-21: Grammar TOML parsing, detect tool matching, action resolution, category resolution,
dangerous pattern detection, rule evaluation order, summary template formatting,
inherit resolution, fallback action, empty grammar handling

## Spec Source
GRAMMARS.md (schema, file format), PROXY.md (categories, dangerous patterns)
close_reason: Grammar system + Categories complete. 33 tests (22 grammar + 11 categories). TOML loading, tool detection, category resolution all working.

title: Step 4: Squasher Pipeline
close_reason: Implemented 7-module squasher pipeline: utf8, vte_strip, progress, pattern, dedup (9 tokenization patterns), truncate, pipeline orchestration. 72 tests all passing.

title: Step 5: Classifier + Emit Buffer
description:
## Task
Implement 3-tier classification engine and emit buffer with flush triggers.

## Files
- src/core/classifier.rs
- src/core/emit.rs

## Classification Types
Classification: Hazard { severity, text, captures }, Outcome { text, captures },
  Noise { action: NoiseAction }, Prompt { text }, Unknown { text }
Severity: Error, Warning
NoiseAction: Strip, Dedup

## Classifier State Machine (8 states)
IDLE -> RUNNING (first line) -> MAYBE_PROMPT (silence >500ms) -> RUNNING (output resumes)
RUNNING -> AWAITING_INPUT (prompt pattern + Partial line) -> RUNNING (stdin received)
RUNNING -> DONE_SUCCESS (exit 0) / DONE_FAILURE (exit !=0) / DONE_KILLED (signal)
RUNNING -> CONSUMING_TRACE (multiline hazard) -> RUNNING (non-matching line)

## 3-Tier Evaluation
Tier 1: Grammar rules (hazard -> outcome -> noise, first match wins)
Tier 2: Universal patterns (always active):
  - ANSI red + error keywords -> Hazard(Error)
  - ANSI yellow + warning keywords -> Hazard(Warning)
  - ANSI green + success keywords -> Outcome
  - Error keywords: ^(error|ERROR|Error)[:s], ^(FAIL|FAILED|FATAL)[:s], etc (17 patterns)
  - Warning keywords: ^(warn|WARN|Warning|WARNING|DEPRECAT)[:s!], \bdeprecated\b, etc
  - Stack traces: Node.js, Python, Go, Rust, Java, generic (12 patterns)
  - Prompts: \?\s*$, \(y\/n\), Password:, Enter.*:, etc (8 patterns)
Tier 3: Structural heuristics:
  - Decorative lines: ^[\s=\-...]{3,}$
  - Edit-distance similarity (threshold 0.3)
  - Volume compression (10+ unknown lines)
  - Temporal heuristics (2s silence)
  - Last 5 lines ring buffer

## EmitBuffer
EmitBuffer { pending_count: u64, outcomes: Vec<CapturedOutcome>, hazards: Vec<EmittedHazard>,
  ring: RingBuffer<String, 5>, line_count: u64, dedup: DedupEngine, start_time: Instant,
  last_emit_time: Instant, output: Vec<String> }

Flush triggers: process exit, hazard detected, prompt detected, silence >2s,
periodic 5s timer, pending >500 lines

TimingConfig { partial_line_timeout: 500ms, silence_timeout: 2s,
  flush_interval: 5s, flush_debounce: 200ms }

## Tests
58 tests covering: all 3 tiers, state machine transitions, emit buffer flush triggers,
hazard immediate emit, outcome accumulation, noise counting, ring buffer,
universal pattern matching, structural heuristics, timing behavior

## Spec Source
CLASSIFIER.md (full spec), STREAMING.md (emit buffer)
close_reason: Implemented 3-tier classifier (49 tests) and EmitBuffer (23 tests). All 202 tests pass.

title: Step 6: Condense Handler
description:
## Task
Wire up condense handler: command -> grammar -> squasher -> condensed output.

## Files
- src/handlers/condense.rs

## What It Does
The condense handler is the full pipeline integration:
1. Spawn command in PTY (Step 2)
2. Load grammar if available (Step 3)
3. Feed PTY output through line buffer -> classifier -> emit buffer (Steps 4-5)
4. On exit, finalize and return condensed summary

Uses the Event enum from STREAMING.md:
Event: PtyOutput(Vec<u8>), StdinInput(Vec<u8>), ChildExited(ExitStatus),
  PartialLineTimeout, SilenceTimeout, FlushTimer, Signal(Signal)

## Event Loop
fn run_condense(command, grammar, config) -> Result<Summary>
  - Spawn PTY, create LineBuffer, Classifier, EmitBuffer
  - Loop: poll events, dispatch to appropriate handler
  - On ChildExited: drain, finalize, return Summary

## Tests
8 tests: condense of known tool (with grammar), condense of unknown tool (no grammar),
exit code handling, hazard passthrough, timeout behavior, signal forwarding,
empty output, very long output

## Spec Source
ARCHITECTURE.md (squasher pipeline), STREAMING.md (event loop)
close_reason: Condense handler: PTY→LineBuffer→Classifier→EmitBuffer pipeline with 8 tests

title: Step 7: Stat Primitives + Narrate Handler
description:
## Task
Implement file stat primitives and narration engine for file operations.

## Files
- src/core/stat.rs
- src/handlers/narrate.rs

## Stat Types
PreFlightInfo { source_size: Option<u64>, source_mtime: Option<SystemTime>,
  source_permissions: Option<u32>, dest_exists: bool, dest_size: Option<u64>, dest_mtime: Option<SystemTime> }
PostFlightInfo { dest_size: Option<u64>, dest_mtime: Option<SystemTime>,
  size_match: bool, file_count: Option<u64>, total_bytes: Option<u64> }
NarratedResult { success: bool, message: String, exit_code: i32 }

## Narration Functions
Per-command narrators for: cp, mv, rm, mkdir, chmod, chown, ln, touch, rmdir
Generic fallback: narrate_generic(command, args, status) -> String
Helper: file_info(path) -> String, human_size(bytes) -> String, gather_tree_info(path) -> TreeInfo

## Narration Format
Success: "-> {cmd}: {source} -> {dest} ({size}, {note})"
Error: "! {cmd}: {source} -> {dest} -- failed (exit {code})"

## Tests
17 tests: narrate cp success/failure/overwrite, narrate rm file/dir/recursive,
narrate mkdir single/nested, narrate chmod, narrate mv, narrate ln symlink,
generic fallback, human_size formatting, file_info for nonexistent

## Spec Source
VERBOSITY.md (stat primitives), PROXY.md (narrate category, narration engine)
close_reason: Stat primitives + narrate handler: 10 stat + 28 narrate tests

title: Step 8: Passthrough + Structured Handlers
description:
## Task
Implement passthrough and structured category handlers.

## Files
- src/handlers/passthrough.rs
- src/handlers/structured.rs

## Passthrough Handler
Execute command, pass output through verbatim, append metadata footer.
Footer format: "-- {lines} lines, {size} --"
Commands: cat, head, tail, grep, ls, echo, etc.

## Structured Handler
Execute with machine-readable flags, parse output, format structured response.
handle_git_status: inject --porcelain=v2, parse lines, count modified/added/deleted/untracked
handle_docker_ps: inject --format json, parse containers
handle_kubectl_get: inject -o json, parse resources

## Tests
9 tests: passthrough output fidelity, metadata footer accuracy,
git status parsing, docker ps parsing, structured fallback to passthrough,
empty output handling

## Spec Source
PROXY.md (Categories 3-4, structured handlers)
close_reason: Passthrough + structured handlers: 7 passthrough + 10 structured tests

title: Step 9: Interactive + Dangerous Handlers
description:
## Task
Implement interactive and dangerous category handlers (CLI mode only for Phase 1).

## Files
- src/handlers/interactive.rs
- src/handlers/dangerous.rs

## Interactive Handler (CLI mode)
Detect raw mode on PTY -> transparent passthrough -> session summary on exit.
Output: "-> {cmd}: session ended ({duration})"

## Dangerous Handler (CLI mode)
Match against dangerous.toml patterns -> terminal warning -> maybe execute.
9 patterns: rm -rf, git push --force, git reset --hard, git clean -f,
docker system prune, DROP TABLE, chmod -R 777, dd
Output: "warning {cmd}: {reason} -- proceed? [y/N]"

## Tests
8 tests: interactive detection, transparent passthrough, session summary,
dangerous pattern matching, warning display, user confirmation flow,
non-dangerous commands pass through, all 9 patterns tested

## Spec Source
PROXY.md (Categories 5-6)
close_reason: Interactive + dangerous handlers: 4 interactive + 4 dangerous tests

title: Phase 3: Core Pipeline Wiring
description: Wire category router, squasher pipeline, and all 6 category handlers into sh_run/sh_spawn. Grammar loading, classifier, emit buffer, dedup, watch patterns, Oreo truncation, preflight, error enrichment. This makes mish output structured, context-efficient responses instead of raw passthrough.

title: Grammar System — TOML parsing, tool detection, action matching
description:
Load TOML grammar files into Grammar structs. Implement detect_tool(args) → Option<(Grammar, Action)>. Support inherit field for shared grammar composition. Rule evaluation order: hazard → outcome → noise. Category resolution: grammar front matter → categories.toml → condense default.

Files: src/core/grammar.rs, grammars/*.toml, grammars/_shared/*.toml, grammars/_meta/categories.toml

Schema: Grammar { tool, detect, inherit, global_noise, actions, fallback, category, quiet, verbosity, enrich }, Action { detect, noise[], hazard[], outcome[], summary }, Rule { pattern, action, severity?, captures[], multiline? }, SummaryTemplate { success, failure, partial }

Tests: TOML parsing for 5 grammars, detect_tool for npm/cargo/git/docker/make, unknown tool returns None, inheritance composition, rule evaluation order, category resolution order.

Grammars to create/validate: npm.toml (install, test, run), cargo.toml (build, test), git.toml (push, pull, clone), docker.toml (build, compose up), make.toml (fallback). Shared: ansi-progress.toml, node-stacktrace.toml, python-traceback.toml, c-compiler-output.toml.
close_reason: Grammar system: evaluate_line, resolve_category, inheritance fix, 43 tests

title: Dangerous Handler — pattern matching + structured warning
description:
Mode-aware handler for destructive commands. Matches patterns from grammars/_meta/dangerous.toml.

Files: src/handlers/dangerous.rs, grammars/_meta/dangerous.toml (already exists)

Patterns: rm -rf, git push --force, git reset --hard, git clean -f, docker system prune, DROP TABLE, chmod -R 777, dd.

MCP mode (basic, before policy engine): Return structured warning with command details, pattern matched, and reason. LLM gets warning and can choose to proceed.

CLI mode: "⚠ {description} — proceed? [y/N]" terminal prompt.

Later (Phase 4): Policy engine integration adds forbidden/auto_confirm/yield_to_operator routing.

Tests: "rm -rf /"→dangerous. "git push --force"→dangerous with reason. "rm file.txt" (no -rf)→NOT dangerous. "dd if=..."→dangerous.
close_reason: Implemented: 9 dangerous patterns, mode-aware CLI prompt / MCP warning. 6 tests passing.

title: Watch Patterns — regex filtering + @presets
description:
Regex filtering of command output for sh_run watch parameter.

Files: src/squasher/pattern.rs

Presets: @errors="error|ERR\!|fatal|panic|exception|traceback", @warnings="warn|deprecat", @test_results="passed|failed|error|skip|===.*===|TOTAL", @npm="added|ERR\!|WARN|vulnerability|audit"

Preset detection: value starting with @[a-z_]+ → lookup preset. All other values → compile as regex (case-insensitive).

Unmatched parameter: "squash" (default, count unmatched lines) or "drop" (discard entirely).

Integration: Runs in squasher pipeline after VTE stripping, before truncation. Only matching lines pass through.

Tests: Preset resolution. Custom regex. Case-insensitive matching. Unmatched=squash→"... 847 lines (no match)". Unmatched=drop→gone. Invalid regex→error.
close_reason: Watch patterns: WatchFilter, UnmatchedMode, @test_results, 30 tests

title: Oreo Truncation — head/tail with enriched markers
description:
When output exceeds max_lines (200 default), keep head + tail with enriched marker in middle.

Files: src/squasher/truncate.rs

Oreo split: 50 head / 150 tail (configurable via head/tail params on sh_run).

Enriched marker: "... [847 lines truncated — 3 errors, 12 warnings in hidden region] ..." Marker includes counts of hazards detected in the truncated middle section.

Max bytes: 64KB hard limit.

Tests: 50-line output→no truncation. 300-line output→50 head + marker + 150 tail. Custom head/tail split. Enriched marker with hazard counts. Max bytes truncation.
close_reason: Oreo truncation: enriched markers, 50/150 split, 64KB limit, 24 tests

title: Preflight Engine — bidirectional argument injection
description:
Bidirectional argument injection: reduce verbose tools (quiet), enrich terse tools (verbose).

Files: src/core/preflight.rs

Quiet injection: Grammar declares [quiet] safe_inject=["--no-progress"] and recommend=["--loglevel=warn"]. Only in summary mode. Never inject if already present. Never inject behavior-changing flags.

Verbose injection: Grammar declares [verbosity] inject=["-v"]. For narrate-category commands (cp -v, rm -v). Dont double-inject.

Pre/post-flight stat: Shared with stat.rs. PreFlightInfo {source_size, source_mtime, dest_exists, dest_size}. PostFlightInfo {dest_size, dest_mtime, size_match, file_count}.

Returns recommendations for LLM: [{flag, reason}].

Tests: npm install→injects --no-progress. cp→injects -v. Already has flag→no double injection. Passthrough mode→no injection. Recommendations returned.
close_reason: Implemented: quiet/verbose injection, passthrough skip, per-action overrides. 15 tests passing.

title: Error Enrichment — diagnostic context on failure
description:
On command failure (exit != 0), gather read-only diagnostics in <100ms.

Files: src/core/enrich.rs

Diagnostic strategies:
- Path resolution: walk path, find break point, list siblings at last valid dir
- Source/target existence: stat source + dest + dest parent
- Permission checks: owner/group/mode vs running user, describe gap
- Command not found (exit 127): check PATH, suggest package from packages.toml
- Exit code semantics: 126=permission denied, 127=not found, 137=OOM, 139=segfault, 130=SIGINT

Per-category enrichment:
- File ops: source_exists, dest_path_walk, permissions
- Git: is_git_repo, branch_exists, branch_list_similar, ahead_behind
- Build: confirm referenced files exist
- Network: port_listening (10ms timeout, non-blocking connect)

Budget: Max 100ms total. Each diagnostic tagged with cost (instant<1ms, fast<10ms, moderate<100ms). Pick in declaration order, skip if over budget.

Grammar integration: [enrich] on_failure=["source_exists","dest_path_walk","permissions"], [enrich.args] source="positional[0..-1]", dest="positional[-1]"

Output format:
! mv: file.txt → /opt/data/ — no such directory (exit 1)
  source:  file.txt (4.2KB ✓)
  path:    /opt/ ✓  /opt/data/ ✗

Tests: Path walk. Source/dest existence. Permission denied. Command not found→package hint. Budget enforcement. OOM (137).
close_reason: Implemented: path walk, file-op diagnosis, permission checks, command-not-found, exit code semantics, budget enforcement. 25 tests passing.

title: sh_run Integration — wire category router into sh_run
description:
Wire sh_run to use the category router instead of raw passthrough.

Files: src/tools/sh_run.rs

Current: sh_run executes command, returns raw output.
Target: sh_run → detect tool → category router → handler → formatted response.

sh_run parameters needing integration:
- watch → pass to squasher/pattern.rs
- unmatched → "squash" or "drop" mode
- head/tail → pass to Oreo truncation
- digest → process table (already works)

Response structure: { result: { cmd, exit_code, output (now categorized), cwd, duration_ms, lines, category, watch_matches, recommendations }, processes: [...] }

Tests: sh_run condense→condensed output. sh_run narrate→narrated. sh_run passthrough→output+footer. sh_run structured→summary. sh_run interactive(MCP)→error. sh_run dangerous→warning. sh_run with watch→filtered. sh_run with head/tail→truncated.
close_reason: Implemented: sh_run wired with category router, squasher for condense, ANSI strip for others, watch patterns, deny-list, dangerous blocking. 28 tests passing.

title: Grammar Test Fixtures — captured output for all 5 grammars
description:
Real captured terminal output for testing grammars.

Files:
- tests/fixtures/npm_install.txt — realistic npm install (1000+ lines with progress, warnings, outcome)
- tests/fixtures/npm_install_error.txt — ERESOLVE, permission errors
- tests/fixtures/cargo_build.txt — cargo build with warnings
- tests/fixtures/cargo_build_error.txt — compiler errors with multiline context
- tests/fixtures/cargo_test.txt — test suite with pass/fail
- tests/fixtures/git_push.txt — push output (noise + ref update)
- tests/fixtures/git_push_rejected.txt — non-fast-forward rejection
- tests/fixtures/docker_build.txt — BuildKit output with cached/done layers
- tests/fixtures/make_build.txt — make with compiler echo lines
- tests/fixtures/ansi_output.txt — output with ANSI color codes
- tests/fixtures/progress_bar.txt — CR-based progress bars
- tests/fixtures/cursor_up_progress.txt — cursor-up multi-line progress (cargo-style)

Tests per grammar: Parse fixture through classifier→verify correct classifications. Run through full condense pipeline→verify condensed output matches expected format. Error fixtures→verify hazards detected.

Each of the 5 grammars must have at least 2 fixtures (success + error). Fixtures should be real captured output, not synthetic.
close_reason: 13 fixtures (2+ per grammar), 27 pipeline tests, Makefile with clean-zombies

title: Category Router — command dispatch to handlers
description:
Top-level routing: command → grammar lookup → categorize → dispatch to handler. Category enum: Condense, Narrate, Passthrough, Structured, Interactive, Dangerous. ExecutionMode enum: Cli, Mcp (for mode-aware handlers).

Files: src/router/mod.rs (route fn), src/router/categories.rs (categorize fn)

Resolution order: grammar front matter category field → categories.toml mapping → condense default. Mode-aware dispatch: Interactive/Dangerous behave differently in CLI vs MCP mode.

Tests: npm install→Condense, cp→Narrate, cat→Passthrough, git status→Structured, vim→Interactive, rm -rf /→Dangerous, unknown→Condense default, MCP mode Interactive→error.
close_reason: ExecutionMode enum + mode-aware dispatch for Interactive/Dangerous. 31 tests.

title: Classifier Engine — three-tier line classification
description:
Three-tier classification: grammar rules → universal patterns → structural heuristics. Each line gets a Classification: Hazard(severity, text, captures), Outcome(text, captures), Noise(Strip|Dedup), Prompt(text), Unknown(text).

Files: src/core/classifier.rs

Tier 1 (Grammar): Evaluate action hazard→outcome→noise rules. First match wins. Global noise after action-specific. Inherited globals last. Multiline hazard: multiline=N attaches next N lines unconditionally.

Tier 2 (Universal): ANSI color + content (red+error→Hazard, yellow+warn→Warning). Error keywords (^error, ^FAIL, ENOENT, segfault). Warning keywords (^warn, deprecated). Stack trace detection (Node/Python/Go/Rust/Java patterns → CONSUMING_TRACE state). Prompt detection (? + Partial line type).

Tier 3 (Structural): Decorative lines (===, ---) → Noise(Strip). Edit-distance similarity (<0.3) → Noise(Dedup). Volume compression (10+ consecutive Unknown → count). Temporal: lines after 2s silence get noise immunity. Ring buffer of last 5 lines for unknown tools.

State machine: IDLE→RUNNING→MAYBE_PROMPT→RUNNING, RUNNING→AWAITING_INPUT, RUNNING→CONSUMING_TRACE→RUNNING, RUNNING→DONE_SUCCESS|DONE_FAILURE.

Tests: Each tier with specific examples. Hazards NEVER suppressed by noise. Multiline consumption. Prompt detection requires Partial line type. State machine transitions.
close_reason: Classifier: multiline hazard, stack traces, edit-distance, temporal bypass, 83 tests

title: Emit Buffer — accumulate, flush, finalize
description:
Accumulate classified lines, emit on flush triggers, build final summary. EmitBuffer holds pending_count, outcomes, hazards, ring buffer (last 5), line_count, dedup engine ref, output vec.

Files: src/core/emit.rs

Accept logic: Hazard → flush pending + emit immediately. Outcome → stored. Noise(Strip) → counted. Noise(Dedup) → to dedup engine. Prompt → flush + emit. Unknown → counted.

Flush triggers: Process exit, Hazard detected, Prompt detected, Silence >2s, Periodic 5s timer, Pending >500 lines.

Finalize: header "{lines} → exit {code} ({time})" + summary lines (grammar template with {var} placeholders, or outcomes as-is, or ring buffer last-lines fallback) + inline hazard lines.

Pending count only shown when ≥10 (noise for small counts).

Tests: Hazard always immediate, flushes pending first. Outcomes stored for finalize. Noise counted. Ring buffer fallback. Grammar summary template with captures. Output format matches spec.
close_reason: Implemented: EmitBuffer with accept logic, flush triggers, ring buffer, finalize. 23 tests passing.

title: Condense Handler — full squasher pipeline integration
description:
The core handler for verbose commands (npm install, cargo build, etc). Wires the full squasher pipeline: PTY spawn → raw bytes → UTF-8 decode → VTE strip → line buffer → progress detection → classifier → dedup → emit buffer → Oreo truncation → structured output.

Files: src/handlers/condense.rs

Integration points: core/pty.rs, squasher/utf8.rs, squasher/vte_strip.rs, core/line_buffer.rs, squasher/progress.rs, core/classifier.rs, squasher/dedup.rs, core/emit.rs, squasher/truncate.rs, squasher/pattern.rs (watch filtering).

The condense handler is the pipeline orchestrator. It creates the classifier (with grammar if detected), creates the emit buffer, runs the event loop (PtyOutput → line_buffer.ingest → classify → emit_buffer.accept), handles flush timers, and calls finalize on process exit.

Tests: npm install fixture → "1400 lines → exit 0 (12.3s) + 147 packages installed ~ npm warn deprecated (x3)". Cargo build fixture → "built in {time}" + errors. Unknown tool → last-lines fallback. Watch pattern active → only matching lines.
close_reason: Implemented: full condense pipeline (PTY→LineBuffer→Classifier→EmitBuffer→Summary). 8 PTY tests passing.

title: Narrate Handler — file operation narration + stat primitives
description:
Narration for file operations. Pre-flight stat → execute (with -v injection) → post-flight stat → one-line narration.

Files: src/handlers/narrate.rs, src/core/stat.rs

Per-command narrators:
- cp: "→ cp: src → dst (size, ok|overwritten)"
- mv: "→ mv: src → dst (size)"
- rm: "→ rm: target (count files, size, removed)"
- mkdir: "→ mkdir: created path (N dirs)"
- chmod: "→ chmod: file old → new"
- chown: "→ chown: file old_owner → new_owner"
- ln: "→ ln: target → link (symlink)"
- touch: "→ touch: file (created|updated)"
- Generic fallback: "→ cmd: ok" or "\! cmd: failed"

Stat primitives (src/core/stat.rs): file_info(path)→String, human_size(bytes)→String, gather_tree_info(path)→{file_count, total_bytes}, get_permissions(path)→String.

Pre-flight: stat source/dest before execution.
Post-flight: stat targets after, check size match for copies.

Tests: cp existing→narration with size. cp overwrite→"overwritten". rm -rf dir→file count + total size. mkdir -p→dir count. chmod→old→new. Failed cp→error narration.
close_reason: Implemented: 8 per-command narrators with pre/post-flight stat primitives. ~30 tests passing.

title: Passthrough Handler — execute + metadata footer
description:
Execute command, pass output through verbatim, append metadata footer.

Files: src/handlers/passthrough.rs

Format: [command output verbatim]\n── {lines} lines, {size} ──

Commands: cat, head, tail, grep, find, ls, echo, printf, wc, sort, uniq, diff, jq, awk, sed, tree, file, stat, which, date, whoami, pwd, id, uname, env, printenv, hostname.

Tests: cat file→contents + footer. ls -la→output + entry count footer. grep→output + match count footer.
close_reason: Implemented: verbatim output + metadata footer. 8 tests passing.

title: Structured Handler — git status, docker ps
description:
Commands with well-known output formats. Inject machine-readable flags, parse, format rich summary.

Files: src/handlers/structured.rs

Handlers:
- git status: inject --porcelain=v2, parse, format "→ git status: N files (M modified, A added, ? untracked)"
- git diff: inject --stat, summarize changes
- docker ps: inject --format json, parse "→ docker ps: N containers running"
- kubectl get: inject -o json, parse (stretch)

Fallback: if no structured handler exists for a command in the structured category, fall through to passthrough handler.

Tests: git status with mixed changes→structured summary. docker ps→container summary. Unknown structured→passthrough fallback.
close_reason: Implemented: git status porcelain v2, docker ps JSON, generic fallback. 12 tests passing.

title: Interactive Handler — mode-aware interactive command handling
description:
Mode-aware handler for interactive commands (vim, htop, less, etc).

Files: src/handlers/interactive.rs

MCP mode: Return structured error — interactive commands cannot run over MCP stdio.
Response: { "error": "Interactive command vim cannot run in MCP mode. Use sh_spawn for background processes." }

CLI mode: Detect raw mode on PTY → transparent passthrough → session summary on exit: "→ vim: session ended (42s)". CLI mode behavior implemented in Phase 5 (CLI Proxy).

Detection: Check against interactive category list (vim, nvim, nano, emacs, htop, top, tmux, screen, less, man, fzf, tig). Dynamic detection via raw mode on PTY for unlisted programs.

Tests: MCP mode "vim file"→structured error. MCP mode "htop"→error. Non-interactive command→not matched.
close_reason: Implemented: mode-aware handler — MCP error, CLI passthrough. 5 tests passing.
